dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, respond to residential for chest pain with possible stroke.
Good evening, I'm Randy with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
It's my left side, all day. Feels like another stroke.
Mr. Smith, can you open your eyes for me? What is your name?
My eyes… Uh, I think they're open. I'm James Smith.
Can you tell me the date today?
Not sure… Thursday, maybe?
Squeeze my pen with your right hand and then your left.
(squeezes both hands equally) There.
Are you having trouble breathing or coughing up mucus?
He's sitting forward, away from the chair.

dialogue.micro_intent: check breathing - Rate and depth of respirations
dialogue.utterance: Breathing is 30, deep and tachypneic. Seems anxious.
SpO2 is 99%, then 97%. Stable.
Capnography is showing an EtCO2 of 14.

dialogue.micro_intent: check circulation - Pulse rate and quality
dialogue.utterance: Pulse is 83, strong and regular.
No cyanosis or diaphoresis. Looks warm.
Blood pressure is 174/86. Stable with recheck.
Have you experienced any sudden weakness, slurred speech, or confusion?
No slurrin' or droop. But I can't move my legs much.
Any allergies to medications, Mr. Smith?
I'm allergic to gabapentin.
What medicines have you taken recently?
He takes Coreg, Plavix, and Lipitor.
You mentioned a prior stroke. What were the symptoms after that?
Slurred speech and my left hand didn't move right.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: When did the left-side pain start today?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Since morning. My wife said I was having a stroke this afternoon.
When exactly did the pain on your left side begin?
Maybe 30 minutes ago. Before lunch.
How would you describe the pain? Sharp, throbbing, or other?
Like an ache, similar to my hip after a fall.
Does it go into your arm, chest or other areas?
No, it's on my left side, all the way down.
On a scale of 0-10, how severe is the pain?
I'd rate it a 4. It's annoying, not the worst.
Pulse now is 87, regular and strong.
Respirations are up to 34, still deep and tachypneic.
BP remains around 174/79.
SpO2 is 97% on room air.
Blood glucose is 216-high, but no acute concern.
EKG is showing sinus rhythm with changes indicative of pulmonary disease.
Let's place an IV in your left arm for future treatments, Mr. Smith.
IV successfully inserted in left AC. Flow appears good.
Next, a 12-lead ECG. Will you lie back slightly for this?
ECG done in supine position, sinus rhythm.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Pupils are equal and reactive to light.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status - Short & Long term memory
dialogue.utterance: Tell me your name, address, and the current year?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status - Short & Long term memory
dialogue.utterance: I'm James Smith. Hard to say the year.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status - GCS
dialogue.utterance: Your Glasgow score is 14. Alert eye, verbal, and motor responses.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status - Short-term memory
dialogue.utterance: Repeat these words for me: Apple, Tree, Cloud.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status - Short-term memory
dialogue.utterance: Apple, Tree, Cloud.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status - Long-term memory
dialogue.utterance: When were you born, Mr. Smith?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status - Long-term memory
head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status - Short-term memory
dialogue.utterance: Now, restate the three words again.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status - Short-term memory
dialogue.utterance: Apple, Tree, Cloud.
Mr. Smith, you seem to forget a bit. Are you aware of where you are now?
I think so. I'm in an ambulance, probably.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Gross motor
dialogue.utterance: Can you both raise and lower your legs?

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Gross motor
dialogue.utterance: No lifting above the ground, but equal pull and push.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Sensory deficits
dialogue.utterance: No numbness noted or sensation loss on either side.
Any deformity, swelling or tenderness in the pelvis or hips?
Some soreness, nothing broken or bent.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Grip strength
dialogue.utterance: Squeeze both my fingers tightly and hold.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Grip strength
dialogue.utterance: Squeezing here. It's equal on both sides.
Stroke/TIA protocol in effect. Transport to MRMC for possible CT.
Pulse is still 87, strong and regular.
Tachypneic with 34 breaths per minute.
Capnography remains at 14, stable.
IV and ECG are both functional and no changes noted.
His wife wants to go to MRMC, are you okay with that?
Yes, memorial is our choice.
Preparing stair chair for transfer.
Mr. Smith, I'll move you into the stair chair now. Is that okay?
Okay, just go slow. My legs aren't cooperative.
Secured into the chair. Ready for stretcher.
Ready to move Mr. Smith via the stretcher into the ambulance.
Loaded into the back. All equip on board.
Departing now. Keep you updated on any changes.
Holder alert. Ready for departure.
Departure now, conditions stable, speed adjusted.
En route to MRMC, speed is maintained.
Vitals stable. No changes from last check.
Glucose remains at 216, no requirement for hyperglycemic treatment.
EKG still normal. Continue communications with ER.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Nuchal rigidity
dialogue.utterance: Any neck pain or difficulty moving your head?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Nuchal rigidity
dialogue.utterance: No pain. I can move my head fine.
When did you last eat or drink something?
Tea and sandwich at 11 AM.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Swallowing difficulties
dialogue.utterance: Can you swallow this small sip of water easily?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Swallowing difficulties
dialogue.utterance: No trouble. Goes down fine.

dialogue.utterance: Repeat the phrase quickly now.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Assessment of mental status - Possible TIA
dialogue.utterance: The sky is blue.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Mental confusion - AMS
dialogue.utterance: Sometimes it's hard to remember. Is this due to the pain or something else?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Mental confusion - AMS
dialogue.utterance: Maybe the pain. Hard to say.
Suspect borderline AMS. No immediate stroke signs observed.
No stroke signs found yet. Likely TIA, proceeding safely.
Based on past medical history, we suspect possible CVA.

dialogue.utterance: Despite the tachypnea, respirations are unlabored.
